preamble: Palatal glides may become rounded high vowels under the influence of neighboring labial or rounded sounds.

examples.note: Contextual rounding in specific historical environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
